evidence: The paper uses permutation feature importance (PFI) and Shapley values for post-hoc model interpretability, not impurity-based importance. Section 2.2.2 explicitly describes conditional inference trees as using "a two-step process...selecting the optimal split variable by a set of statistical hypothesis tests before determining the corresponding split rule. In this way, a selection bias towards covariates with many possible splits is avoided." The application section computes PFI solely for descriptive visualization of a fitted model, with the statement that "higher PFI_j values indicate a higher importance of the j-th covariate for estimating the RMST," but makes no causal or ranking-based claims that depend on importance magnitude.
